metadata.title: Women Connect International Training - Empowering Diaspora Women Through Resilience, Leadership & Impact
metadata.description: Women Connect International Training (WCI) strengthens emotional resilience, economic empowerment, and leadership capacity of African women in the diaspora. Based in Doha, Qatar.
metadata.keywords: women empowerment, diaspora women, African women, resilience, leadership, mentoring, psychosocial support, economic empowerment, digital literacy, humanitarian impact, NGO Qatar

slide1.title: Empowering Diaspora Women to Build
slide1.description: Women Connect International Training strengthens the emotional resilience, economic empowerment, and leadership capacity of African women in the diaspora through evidence-based programs and community support.

slide2.title: Developing the Next Generation of
slide2.description: Through mentorship, governance literacy, and cross-border exchange, we equip diaspora women with the tools and confidence to lead transformative change in their communities and beyond.

slide3.title: Transparent Humanitarian Action for
slide3.description: Our pooled-contribution model and real-time transparency dashboard ensure every donation creates measurable impact for vulnerable communities across Africa.

hero.carouselLabel: Hero slideshow showcasing WCI programs and impact

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Empowering Diaspora Women
about.description: Women Connect International Training is an NGO dedicated to strengthening emotional resilience, economic empowerment, and leadership capacity of African women in the diaspora.

hero.subtitle: Women Connect International Training (WCI) is an NGO based in Doha, Qatar, dedicated to empowering African women in the diaspora through psychosocial resilience, digital and economic empowerment, mentoring, and transparent humanitarian impact.

metrics.countriesDesc: From Doha to communities across Africa

description.para1: <company>Women Connect International Training</company> was founded by two women who understood firsthand the challenges diaspora women face: emotional isolation, economic barriers, and limited access to leadership opportunities. What began as a shared vision between a counselling psychologist and a systems strategist has grown into a movement for change.
description.para2: Our founders, <highlight>Fatima Abubakar</highlight> (counselling psychologist with 9+ years of experience) and <highlight>Rakiya Shuaibu Mohammed</highlight> (expert in systems strategy, digital transformation, and governance), created WCI to bridge the gap between <highlight>emotional wellbeing</highlight>, <highlight>economic empowerment</highlight>, <highlight>leadership development</highlight>, and <highlight>transparent humanitarian action</highlight> for African women in the diaspora.

foundation.title: Foundation
foundation.description: WCI was founded in Doha, Qatar with a vision to empower African diaspora women through holistic support programs.

expansion.title: Program Launch
expansion.description: Launched our four core program pillars: Psychosocial Resilience, Digital & Economic Empowerment, Mentoring & Leadership, and Humanitarian Impact.

global.title: Community Growth
global.description: Expanded our reach across multiple diaspora communities, building a connected network of women supporting women.

innovation.title: AI Platform Launch
innovation.description: Introduced our AI-powered platform with emotional support, learning management, mentor matching, and transparency dashboard modules.

missionVision.heading: Driven by Purpose
missionVision.subheading: Our mission and vision guide every program we design, every partnership we form, and every life we touch.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.heading: Strengthening Resilience, Empowering Futures
mission.description: To strengthen the emotional resilience, economic empowerment, and leadership capacity of African women in the diaspora through evidence-based psychosocial support, digital and economic skills training, mentorship programs, and transparent humanitarian initiatives that create lasting impact in vulnerable communities across Africa.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.heading: A Globally Connected Ecosystem
vision.description: To build a globally connected ecosystem where diaspora women are empowered as resilient leaders, economic actors, and accountable partners in transforming vulnerable communities across Africa. We envision a world where every African woman in the diaspora has access to the emotional support, economic tools, and leadership pathways she needs to thrive and create lasting change.

values.title: Our Values
values.heading: What We Believe
values.description: Our values are the foundation of everything we do. They shape our programs, guide our partnerships, and define how we serve our community.
values.subheading: These five core values are embedded in every program, platform, and partnership we build. They reflect who we are and what we stand for.

innovation.title: Dignity
innovation.desc: We honour the inherent worth and lived experience of every woman we serve, creating safe spaces where vulnerability is met with respect and compassion.

reliability.title: Accountability
reliability.desc: We hold ourselves to the highest standards of transparency in governance, finances, and impact measurement, ensuring trust with our community and partners.

integrity.title: Equity
integrity.desc: We are committed to dismantling barriers and creating fair access to opportunities, resources, and support for all diaspora women regardless of background.

teamwork.title: Innovation
teamwork.desc: We embrace technology and creative approaches to deliver scalable, accessible programs that meet women where they are, leveraging AI and digital tools for greater impact.

excellence.title: Collaboration
excellence.desc: We believe lasting change happens through partnership. We work alongside communities, organisations, and individuals to co-create solutions that serve diaspora women.

growth.title: Resilience
growth.desc: We model and cultivate the resilience we seek to build in others, adapting to challenges and growing stronger together through every experience.

team.heading: Meet the Visionaries
team.subheading: WCI was co-founded by two women united by a shared commitment to empowering African women in the diaspora through holistic, evidence-based programs.

ceo.name: Rakiya Shuaibu Mohammed
ceo.bio: Expert in systems strategy, digital transformation, and governance with deep experience building scalable programs across Africa and the Middle East.
ceo.extendedBio: Rakiya brings extensive expertise in systems strategy, digital innovation, and organisational governance. Her strategic vision drives WCI's platform development, partnership strategy, and operational excellence, ensuring every program is designed for scalability and measurable impact across diaspora communities.

cto.name: Fatima Abubakar
cto.bio: Counselling psychologist with over 9 years of experience, specialising in emotional resilience and wellbeing programs for women and communities.
cto.extendedBio: Fatima is a licensed counselling psychologist with over 9 years of clinical and community experience. She leads WCI's psychosocial resilience programming, designing evidence-based healing circles, emotional intelligence workshops, and peer support frameworks that form the foundation of WCI's trauma-informed approach.

headAnalytics.name: Program Team
headAnalytics.bio: Our growing network of trained facilitators, mentors, and subject matter experts delivers programs across all four pillars.
headAnalytics.extendedBio: WCI's program team includes certified counsellors, digital skills trainers, governance experts, and community leaders who bring diverse expertise and lived experience to every workshop, mentoring session, and community initiative we deliver.

headCybersecurity.name: Advisory Board
headCybersecurity.bio: Our advisory board provides guidance on program design, governance, partnerships, and impact measurement.
headCybersecurity.extendedBio: WCI's advisory board comprises leaders in psychology, international development, technology, and governance who ensure our programs meet the highest standards of evidence-based practice and community accountability.

timeline.subheading: From a shared vision in Doha to a growing ecosystem of empowered women across the globe, our journey is defined by purpose and impact.

foundation.title: WCI Founded
foundation.description: Women Connect International Training was established in Doha, Qatar (C.R. No: 237930) by co-founders Fatima Abubakar and Rakiya Shuaibu Mohammed.

expansion.title: Core Programs Launched
expansion.description: Rolled out our four program pillars: Psychosocial Resilience, Digital & Economic Empowerment, Mentoring & Leadership, and Transparent Humanitarian Impact.

global.title: Community Expansion
global.description: Grew our network across multiple diaspora communities, establishing healing circles, mentoring cohorts, and digital literacy workshops.

innovation.title: AI Platform Development
innovation.description: Began development of our AI-powered platform featuring emotional support, learning management, mentor matching, and humanitarian transparency modules.

future.title: Scaling for Impact
future.description: Expanding partnerships, growing our ambassador program, and building cross-border exchange networks to reach more diaspora women across the globe.

globalPresence.subheading: Based in Doha, Qatar with a growing network of diaspora women and community partners across Africa, the Middle East, and beyond.

cta.description: Whether you want to participate in our programs, become a mentor, volunteer your skills, or support our humanitarian projects, there is a place for you at WCI.

reason1.title: Holistic Approach
reason1.description: We address emotional, economic, and leadership needs together because true empowerment requires support across all dimensions of a woman's life.

reason2.title: Lived Experience
reason2.description: Our founders and facilitators understand the diaspora experience firsthand, bringing empathy and cultural competence to every program.

reason3.title: Radical Transparency
reason3.description: Our humanitarian transparency dashboard ensures every contribution is tracked, every project is visible, and every impact is measured.

reason4.title: AI-Powered Platform
reason4.description: Our intelligent platform provides personalised emotional support, skills training, mentor matching, and impact tracking at scale.

services.label: What We Do
services.title: Our Programs
services.subtitle: Four Pillars of Empowerment
services.description: Our four core program pillars work together to provide holistic support for African women in the diaspora, addressing emotional resilience, economic empowerment, leadership development, and humanitarian impact.

businessProcessManagement.name: Psychosocial Resilience
businessProcessManagement.description: Healing circles, emotional intelligence training, burnout prevention workshops, peer support forums, and professional referral pathways to strengthen emotional wellbeing and community resilience.

cyberSecurity.name: Digital & Economic Empowerment
cyberSecurity.description: Digital literacy training, AI tools for productivity, entrepreneurship bootcamps, financial literacy workshops, and remote work readiness programs to build economic independence.

blockchain.name: Mentoring & Leadership
blockchain.description: AI-powered mentor matching, governance literacy training, advocacy skills development, ambassador programs, and cross-border exchange opportunities to develop the next generation of women leaders.

itConsultation.name: Transparent Humanitarian Impact
itConsultation.description: Pooled community contributions, participatory project selection, real-time transparency dashboards, and rigorous impact measurement to ensure accountable, effective humanitarian action.

cybersecurity.name: Psychosocial Resilience & Wellbeing
cybersecurity.description: Evidence-based emotional support programs including healing circles, emotional intelligence training, burnout prevention, and peer support communities.

aiData.name: Digital & Economic Empowerment
aiData.description: Building digital skills, financial literacy, entrepreneurship capacity, and remote work readiness for economic independence.

blockchain.name: Mentoring & Leadership Development
blockchain.description: Connecting women with mentors, building governance literacy, and developing advocacy and leadership skills across borders.

itGovernance.name: Transparent Humanitarian Impact
itGovernance.description: Ensuring every contribution creates measurable change through transparent project selection, tracking, and impact reporting.

cta.title: Ready to Start Your Empowerment Journey?
cta.description: Join our community of diaspora women building resilience, developing skills, and creating lasting impact together.

products.label: Our Platform
products.title: AI-Powered Empowerment Platform
products.subtitle: Four intelligent modules designed to support your journey from resilience to leadership to impact.
products.description: Our AI-powered platform provides personalised support across four integrated modules, meeting women where they are and empowering them to grow.

trustMe.name: Emotional Support Companion
trustMe.tagline: Your Safe Space for Guided Self-Reflection
trustMe.description: A non-diagnostic AI companion that provides guided emotional check-ins, mood tracking, resilience exercises, and personalised wellbeing resources. It helps women process their experiences in a safe, confidential environment and connects them with professional support when needed.

digiGate.name: Learning Management Hub
digiGate.tagline: Structured Pathways to Resilience and Economic Power
digiGate.description: A comprehensive learning platform offering structured curricula in psychosocial resilience, digital literacy, entrepreneurship, financial management, and leadership. Features self-paced courses, interactive workshops, progress tracking, and certification pathways.

digiTrack.name: AI Mentor Matching Engine
digiTrack.tagline: Connecting You with the Right Mentor at the Right Time
digiTrack.description: An intelligent matching system that pairs women with mentors based on skills, goals, availability, language, and cultural background. Facilitates structured mentoring relationships with goal-setting, progress tracking, and feedback loops.

trustMeHub.name: Humanitarian Transparency Dashboard
trustMeHub.tagline: See Where Every Contribution Goes. Track Every Impact.
trustMeHub.description: A real-time dashboard that tracks pooled community contributions, project selection processes, fund allocation, implementation progress, and measurable outcomes. Ensures radical transparency and accountability in all humanitarian initiatives.

boaCRM.name: Community Hub
boaCRM.tagline: Your Gateway to the WCI Ecosystem
boaCRM.description: A centralised community platform connecting all WCI members, facilitating peer support, event coordination, resource sharing, and cross-border networking among diaspora women worldwide.

training.title: Training Programs
training.subtitle: Build Your Skills and Confidence
training.description: WCI offers structured training programs designed to build emotional resilience, digital skills, economic independence, and leadership capacity for diaspora women.

faqs.supportMessage: Our team is here to help

services1.question: What programs does Women Connect International Training offer?
services1.answer: WCI offers four core program pillars: (1) Psychosocial Resilience - including healing circles, emotional intelligence training, and burnout prevention; (2) Digital & Economic Empowerment - including digital literacy, AI tools training, entrepreneurship, and financial literacy; (3) Mentoring & Leadership - including AI-powered mentor matching, governance literacy, and advocacy skills; and (4) Transparent Humanitarian Impact - including pooled community contributions with full transparency and impact measurement.

services2.question: Who is eligible to participate in WCI programs?
services2.answer: WCI programs are designed primarily for African women living in the diaspora. Whether you are a recent migrant, a long-term resident abroad, a student, or a professional, our programs are tailored to support your unique journey. We welcome women of all backgrounds, ages, and experience levels who identify with the African diaspora experience.

training.question: How does the AI-powered platform work?
training.answer: Our platform features four integrated modules: (1) The Emotional Support Companion provides non-diagnostic guided check-ins and wellbeing resources; (2) The Learning Management Hub offers structured courses in resilience and economic skills; (3) The AI Mentor Matching Engine connects you with mentors based on your goals, skills, and preferences; and (4) The Humanitarian Transparency Dashboard lets you track project progress and impact in real time. All modules work together to provide a holistic support experience.

different.question: What makes WCI different from other women's organisations?
different.answer: WCI is unique in our holistic, four-pillar approach that integrates emotional wellbeing, economic empowerment, leadership development, and humanitarian impact into one connected ecosystem. We are founded by diaspora women for diaspora women, we leverage AI technology to scale personalised support, and we maintain radical transparency in all our humanitarian initiatives through our real-time dashboard.

gettingStarted.question: How do I get started with WCI?
gettingStarted.answer: Getting started is simple. You can join our community by registering on our platform, attending one of our open healing circles or workshops, or reaching out to us through the contact page. We will help you identify the programs and resources that best match your needs and goals. There is no cost to join our community, and many of our foundational programs are offered free of charge.

international.question: I live outside Qatar. Can I still participate?
international.answer: Absolutely! While WCI is headquartered in Doha, Qatar, our programs are designed to serve diaspora women wherever they are. Most of our workshops, healing circles, and training programs are available online. Our AI platform is accessible from anywhere in the world. We also facilitate cross-border exchange programs and have a growing network of community ambassadors across multiple countries.

cta.title: Still have questions?
cta.subtitle: Cannot find the answer you are looking for? Reach out to our team.

testimonial1.quote: The healing circles gave me a space to breathe and process what I had been carrying alone for years. For the first time since leaving home, I felt truly seen and understood by women who share my experience.
testimonial1.author: Amina K.

testimonial2.quote: The digital skills training transformed my outlook completely. I went from feeling lost in a new country to launching my own online business. WCI did not just teach me skills, they gave me confidence.
testimonial2.author: Grace O.

testimonial3.quote: Being matched with my mentor through the platform changed everything. She understood my background and helped me navigate governance and advocacy in ways I never thought possible. I am now leading community initiatives.
testimonial3.author: Fatou D.

testimonial4.quote: What I love most about WCI is the transparency. I can see exactly where my contributions go and the real impact they make in communities back home. It restored my trust in giving back.
testimonial4.author: Zainab M.

testimonial5.quote: The emotional support companion on the platform became my daily anchor. The guided check-ins helped me recognise burnout before it consumed me. Every diaspora woman needs this kind of support.
testimonial5.author: Chioma A.

testimonial6.quote: WCI's entrepreneurship bootcamp gave me the financial literacy and business planning skills I needed to turn my idea into a real business. The community of women cheering me on made all the difference.
testimonial6.author: Halima B.

process.label: Our Approach
process.title: The WCI Journey
process.subtitle: Our proven four-step journey guides women from emotional stabilisation through skill-building, leadership development, and community impact

step1.title: Stabilise
step1.description: Begin with psychosocial support, healing circles, and emotional resilience building. Create a foundation of emotional wellbeing and community belonging that enables growth in all other areas.

step2.title: Strengthen
step2.description: Build digital literacy, economic skills, and financial independence through structured training programs. Develop the practical capabilities needed to thrive in the diaspora and contribute to your community.

step3.title: Lead
step3.description: Step into leadership through mentorship, governance training, and advocacy development. Connect with mentors, join the ambassador program, and develop the confidence to lead change at every level.

step4.title: Impact
step4.description: Channel your growth into transparent humanitarian action. Contribute to community projects, participate in impact measurement, and help transform vulnerable communities across Africa with full accountability.
